input_files_consumed: risk_factors.json (46 risks: 5 CRITICAL, 10 HIGH, 29 MEDIUM, 2 LOW)
metadata.measurement_philosophy: Leading indicators trigger decisions before lagging indicators confirm damage. Every Tier 1 opportunity has at least 2 metrics (1 leading, 1 lagging). Every Tier 2 opportunity has at least 1 metric. Thresholds are calibrated to a DTC premium golf equipment launch targeting 5-15 handicap frustrated ball-strikers via a $399/$429 price point.

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Own the 'Active Face Control' category with Forward Weighting and face-down balance demonstration

metrics.metric_name: Let It Fall Demo Video Completion Rate
metrics.measurement_method: Track percentage of viewers who watch the face-down balance demonstration video to 75%+ completion across all platforms (YouTube, landing page embed, social ads)
metrics.target_threshold: >65% completion rate to 75% mark
metrics.failure_threshold: <50% completion rate — demo is not compelling enough to hold attention
metrics.data_source: YouTube Analytics, Wistia/landing page video hosting analytics, Meta Ads Manager video metrics

metrics.measurement_method: Track branded search volume for 'forward weighting putter,' 'face down balance putter,' 'RS1 putter,' and 'active face control' via Google Search Console and Google Trends relative index
metrics.target_threshold: >500 branded searches/week by Week 4 post-launch
metrics.failure_threshold: <200 searches/week by Week 4 — category messaging is not penetrating
metrics.data_source: Google Search Console, Google Trends, SEMrush/Ahrefs branded keyword tracking

metrics.measurement_method: Track the conversion rate from viewers who watched the Let It Fall demonstration (75%+ completion) to add-to-cart action on the RS1 product page
metrics.target_threshold: >8% demo-viewer to add-to-cart
metrics.failure_threshold: <4% — demo is generating interest but not purchase intent; mechanism story may not be connecting to value
metrics.data_source: Shopify/ecommerce analytics with video completion event tracking, Google Analytics 4 funnel

metrics.metric_name: Face-Down Balance Mention Rate in UGC/Reviews
metrics.measurement_method: Track percentage of customer reviews, social mentions, and forum posts about RS1 that reference the face-down balance test, forward weighting, or active face control in their own words
metrics.target_threshold: >40% of organic mentions reference the mechanism by Month 3
metrics.failure_threshold: <20% — category vocabulary is not transferring to customers; they are describing RS1 in generic terms
metrics.data_source: Mention.com or Brandwatch social listening, manual review sampling, customer review content analysis

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Capture LAB Golf's lost majority (55-65% of consideration funnel) with face stability minus the tradeoffs

metrics.measurement_method: Track organic and paid traffic arriving at RS1 pages from LAB-related search queries ('LAB Golf alternative,' 'better than LAB Golf,' 'LAB Golf problems,' 'RS1 vs LAB')
metrics.target_threshold: >300 LAB-keyword visits/week by Month 2
metrics.failure_threshold: <100 — comparative positioning is not generating search interest
metrics.data_source: Google Search Console query data, Google Ads search term reports, SEMrush competitive keyword tracking

metrics.measurement_method: Track conversion rate of visitors who enter the funnel from LAB-comparison content or ads specifically targeting LAB's blocker segments (aesthetics, price, adaptation period, center-shaft)
metrics.target_threshold: >5% LAB-comparison page visitor to purchase
metrics.failure_threshold: <2% — comparative messaging is attracting curiosity but not closing; may need stronger proof or blocker-specific landing pages
metrics.data_source: Shopify/ecommerce with UTM attribution for LAB-comparison campaigns, landing page analytics

metrics.measurement_method: Include post-purchase survey question: 'Were you considering a LAB Golf putter before purchasing RS1?' Track percentage answering yes
metrics.target_threshold: >25% of RS1 buyers were LAB-considerers
metrics.failure_threshold: <15% — RS1 is not capturing the LAB-adjacent segment; may be attracting a different buyer profile
metrics.data_source: Post-purchase survey (Klaviyo/email automation), customer onboarding flow

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Lead with 'Your stroke was never the problem' self-blame liberation reframe

metrics.measurement_method: Track click-through rate, save rate, and share rate on ads and organic content leading with the 'your stroke was never the problem' reframe vs control ads with standard mechanism messaging
metrics.target_threshold: >2.5% CTR on self-blame reframe ads (vs 1.5% industry average for golf equipment)
metrics.failure_threshold: <1.5% CTR — reframe is not resonating or is alienating; test alternative framings
metrics.data_source: Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, email open/click rates for self-blame subject lines

metrics.measurement_method: Track click-through rate on the specific email in the nurture sequence that delivers the self-blame liberation message, compared to other emails in the sequence
metrics.target_threshold: >4% click rate (top 20% of sequence emails by engagement)
metrics.failure_threshold: <2.5% — liberation message is underperforming the sequence average; reframe may need proof pairing
metrics.data_source: Klaviyo email analytics, sequence step performance comparison

metrics.measurement_method: Track percentage of visitors who arrive at Problem-Aware content (putting frustration, missed putts) and progress to Solution-Aware content (forward weighting mechanism page, demo video) within the same session or within 7 days
metrics.target_threshold: >35% session progression from problem content to solution content
metrics.failure_threshold: <20% — content is generating emotional resonance but failing to bridge to the RS1 solution
metrics.data_source: Google Analytics 4 path analysis, landing page funnel analytics

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Coin and own 'face drift' as the consumer-friendly root cause name

metrics.metric_name: Face Drift Term Adoption in Organic Conversation
metrics.measurement_method: Track mentions of 'face drift' in golf forums (GolfWRX, Reddit r/golf), YouTube comments, and social media posts NOT created by PG. Count organic adoption of the coined term by third parties
metrics.target_threshold: >50 organic third-party mentions by Month 3
metrics.failure_threshold: <20 organic mentions — term is not entering natural vocabulary; may need simpler or more intuitive coinage
metrics.data_source: Social listening tools (Mention, Brandwatch), manual GolfWRX/Reddit monitoring, Google Alerts for 'face drift'

metrics.measurement_method: Track Google search volume for 'face drift' and 'face drift putter' from zero baseline. Measure week-over-week growth rate after launch
metrics.target_threshold: >200 searches/month for 'face drift' by Month 3
metrics.failure_threshold: <50 searches/month — term is not gaining traction in search behavior
metrics.data_source: Google Trends, Google Search Console, Ahrefs/SEMrush keyword tracking

metrics.metric_name: Competitor Co-option of Face Drift Vocabulary
metrics.measurement_method: Monitor competitor marketing materials, social content, and retail copy for adoption of 'face drift' or directly derivative terms. Track time-to-first-competitor-mention
metrics.target_threshold: No competitor adoption in first 6 months (RS1 maintains exclusive ownership)
metrics.warning_threshold: 1 competitor uses the term in first 6 months
metrics.failure_threshold: >1 competitor uses the term within 6 months — vocabulary is being co-opted before ownership is established; accelerate trademark and branding investment
metrics.data_source: Competitor website monitoring, competitive intelligence tools, GolfWRX/Reddit brand mention tracking

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Deploy evolutionary narrative: Traditional -> LAB/Zero Torque -> RS1/Forward Weighting

metrics.measurement_method: Track engagement metrics (time on page, scroll depth, video completion) on the three-generation evolutionary narrative content (email, VSL section, landing page) vs other content sections
metrics.target_threshold: >3 min average time on page for evolutionary narrative content; >80% scroll depth
metrics.warning_threshold: 2-3 min time on page; 60-80% scroll depth
metrics.failure_threshold: <2 min time on page; <60% scroll depth — narrative is too long, too complex, or not compelling
metrics.data_source: Google Analytics 4 engagement metrics, Wistia/video hosting scroll and watch metrics

metrics.measurement_method: Track the three-putter side-by-side comparison video (traditional vs ZT vs RS1) view count, completion rate, and share rate across platforms
metrics.target_threshold: >100K views in first 30 days; >50% completion rate
metrics.failure_threshold: <50K views or <35% completion — comparison format is not engaging or distribution is insufficient
metrics.data_source: YouTube Analytics, social platform native analytics, paid ad video metrics

metrics.metric_name: Evolutionary Frame Recall in Customer Feedback
metrics.measurement_method: In post-purchase surveys and customer interviews, track how many buyers spontaneously reference the evolutionary narrative ('next generation,' 'beyond zero torque,' 'evolution of putting') when asked why they chose RS1
metrics.target_threshold: >30% of surveyed customers reference the evolutionary frame
metrics.failure_threshold: <15% — narrative is not sticky; customers are buying for other reasons and the evolutionary story is not being retained
metrics.data_source: Post-purchase survey, customer interview transcripts, review content analysis

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Name and explain the honeymoon effect to carousel riders as a purchase framework

metrics.measurement_method: Track open rate and click rate on emails and ads that lead with 'Why Every Putter Works for Two Weeks' or honeymoon-effect messaging. Compare to control messaging without the honeymoon hook
metrics.target_threshold: >35% open rate on honeymoon subject lines; >3.5% CTR (vs 25% avg open, 2% avg CTR)
metrics.failure_threshold: <25% open rate — honeymoon framing is not cutting through; test alternative hooks
metrics.data_source: Klaviyo email analytics, Meta Ads Manager engagement metrics

metrics.measurement_method: Track conversion rate of visitors who self-identify as carousel riders (owned 3+ putters in 2 years) via quiz, survey, or segment-specific landing page content. Compare to overall conversion rate
metrics.target_threshold: >6% conversion rate for identified carousel riders (vs ~3% overall)
metrics.failure_threshold: <3% — carousel riders are not converting at premium rates despite targeted messaging; honeymoon explanation may not be sufficient to overcome embedded skepticism
metrics.data_source: Shopify/ecommerce segmented conversion data, quiz/survey segment attribution

metrics.measurement_method: Track the percentage of RS1 buyers who are still using the putter as their primary putter at 90 days post-purchase (via follow-up survey, Arccos data if integrated, or customer service inquiry). This is the ultimate proof that RS1 breaks the carousel
metrics.target_threshold: >85% still using RS1 as primary putter at 90 days
metrics.failure_threshold: <70% — RS1 is experiencing its own honeymoon effect; the mechanism claim is undermined and return rates will spike
metrics.data_source: 90-day post-purchase survey, Arccos integration data (if available), return/exchange rate inverse

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Leverage Chris McGinley's 25-year OEM pedigree as credibility mechanism

metrics.measurement_method: Track view count, completion rate, and engagement metrics (likes, comments, shares) on the Chris McGinley documentary-style origin video across platforms
metrics.target_threshold: >60% completion rate on 8-12 min origin video; >2% engagement rate
metrics.failure_threshold: <40% completion — origin story is too long, not compelling, or not positioned correctly in the funnel
metrics.data_source: YouTube Analytics, landing page video hosting metrics, social platform analytics

metrics.metric_name: Cold Traffic McGinley-Lead vs Brand-Lead A/B Performance
metrics.measurement_method: A/B test cold traffic ads: 'Chris McGinley's RS1' / 'RS1 by McGinley' creative vs 'Performance Golf RS1' creative. Track CTR, CPC, and downstream conversion for each variant
metrics.target_threshold: McGinley-lead ads outperform brand-lead by >30% on CTR
metrics.failure_threshold: McGinley-lead underperforms or is within 10% of brand-lead — designer credibility is not the differentiator assumed; revisit cold traffic strategy
metrics.data_source: Meta Ads Manager A/B test results, Google Ads experiment data

metrics.measurement_method: Post-purchase survey question: 'How important was the designer's (Chris McGinley) background in your purchase decision?' Scale 1-10. Also track mentions of McGinley credentials in customer reviews
metrics.target_threshold: >7.0 average importance score; >20% of reviews mention McGinley/designer credentials
metrics.failure_threshold: <5.0 average — McGinley credibility is not a significant purchase driver; may need to invest more in proof or change credibility strategy
metrics.data_source: Post-purchase survey (Klaviyo), review content analysis, NPS survey verbatim analysis

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Position RS1 as Technology + Technique dual-promise putter

metrics.metric_name: Dual-Promise Content Engagement vs Primary Messaging
metrics.measurement_method: Track engagement metrics on 'Technology + Technique' content (email, social, landing page section) relative to primary face-stability messaging. Measure click-through, time-on-section, and progression to purchase
metrics.target_threshold: Within 80% of primary messaging engagement rates
metrics.failure_threshold: <50% of primary messaging engagement — dual promise is diluting rather than supporting the mechanism story
metrics.data_source: GA4 content group comparison, Klaviyo email A/B results, landing page section heatmap

metrics.metric_name: Stroke Improvement Self-Report Rate at 90 Days
metrics.measurement_method: In 90-day post-purchase survey, ask: 'Have you noticed improvement in your stroke mechanics (not just results) since using RS1?' Track yes/no and qualitative descriptions
metrics.target_threshold: >50% of respondents report perceived stroke improvement
metrics.failure_threshold: <30% — dual-promise claim lacks experiential evidence; deprioritize in messaging
metrics.data_source: 90-day post-purchase survey, customer testimonial content analysis

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Gravity-driven pendulum distance control advantage vs ZT putters

metrics.measurement_method: Track engagement on distance control advantage content (email, social, FAQ section). Measure click rates, time on content, and progression to product page vs other secondary benefit content
metrics.target_threshold: >2.5% CTR on distance control content; >2 min time on page
metrics.failure_threshold: <1.5% CTR — distance control messaging is not resonating; deprioritize until testing data is available
metrics.data_source: Klaviyo email analytics, GA4 content engagement, landing page section analytics

metrics.metric_name: Three-Putt Reduction Rate in Customer Data
metrics.measurement_method: Track self-reported or Arccos-verified three-putt frequency reduction among RS1 buyers at 30, 60, and 90 day intervals post-purchase
metrics.target_threshold: >25% average reduction in three-putts per round by 90 days
metrics.failure_threshold: <10% — distance control claim lacks empirical support; pivot to qualitative 'feel' messaging instead
metrics.data_source: Customer survey, Arccos integration data, beta tester Arccos/ShotScope data

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Target the yips/putting anxiety segment with RS1 as the mechanical antidote

metrics.measurement_method: Track engagement and conversion from content specifically targeting putting anxiety/yips sufferers (email nurture sequences, targeted social ads, dedicated landing page). Compare to general audience conversion
metrics.target_threshold: >4% conversion rate for anxiety-targeted content (premium segment with high emotional urgency)
metrics.failure_threshold: <2% — anxiety segment messaging is attracting clicks but not converting; may need stronger proof or testimonials from yips sufferers
metrics.data_source: Segmented campaign analytics (Klaviyo, Meta), dedicated landing page conversion data

metrics.measurement_method: Track number of unsolicited and solicited testimonials from RS1 buyers who describe yips/anxiety improvement. Count testimonials that specifically mention reduced anxiety, yips improvement, or restored confidence under pressure
metrics.target_threshold: >15 anxiety-resolution testimonials by Month 6
metrics.failure_threshold: <5 — RS1 is not generating meaningful anxiety-resolution stories; deprioritize segment targeting
metrics.data_source: Customer review mining, post-purchase survey anxiety questions, customer success outreach

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Price-wedge positioning: RS1 at $399/$429 undercuts LAB ($449-$649)

metrics.metric_name: Price Comparison Page Engagement and Conversion
metrics.measurement_method: Track visits, time on page, and conversion rate from the value comparison table content (RS1 vs LAB vs Spider ZT vs Scotty vs Bettinardi). Measure whether price comparison content supports or dilutes purchase intent
metrics.target_threshold: >5% conversion rate from price comparison page; >2 min time on page
metrics.failure_threshold: <3% conversion — price comparison is creating analysis paralysis rather than driving purchase; simplify or reposition
metrics.data_source: GA4 landing page analytics, Shopify conversion tracking with UTM attribution

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Deploy schema-violation messaging structures to bypass Stage 4 audience numbness

metrics.metric_name: Schema-Violation Ad Creative Performance vs Standard
metrics.measurement_method: A/B test schema-violation ad formats (demo-first, no brand intro; falsifiable challenge; honeymoon acknowledgment) against standard Problem-Mechanism-Proof-Offer format. Track CTR, cost per click, and cost per acquisition
metrics.target_threshold: Schema-violation creative outperforms standard by >25% on CPA
metrics.failure_threshold: Schema-violation underperforms or is within 10% — audience may prefer familiar formats; standard creative wins; abandon schema violation approach
metrics.data_source: Meta Ads Manager A/B test results, Google Ads experiment data

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Position 74-degree Square-to-Square lie angle as designed-in innovation

metrics.measurement_method: Track engagement on 74-degree lie angle technical content specifically among data-driven / GolfWRX-type audience segments. Measure time on content, shares to golf forums, and click-through to product page
metrics.target_threshold: >3 min time on technical content; >1% share rate
metrics.failure_threshold: <1.5 min — technical audience is not engaging with lie angle content; feature is too niche for content investment
metrics.data_source: GA4 segment analysis (technical audience via referral source), social share tracking

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Build the data proof package (SAM PuttLab, Arccos, independent testing) post-launch

metrics.measurement_method: Track progress of RS1 through independent testing pipeline: submission date, receipt confirmation, testing start date, expected publication date. Monitor for MyGolfSpy, TXG, and other independent reviewers
metrics.target_threshold: MyGolfSpy testing initiated by Month 3; first independent review published by Month 6
metrics.warning_threshold: Testing initiated by Month 6; review published by Month 9
metrics.failure_threshold: No independent testing initiated by Month 6 — RS1 is missing the data-driven audience entirely; investigate submission process and relationships
metrics.data_source: Internal tracking spreadsheet, communication log with testing organizations

metrics.measurement_method: Track conversion rate and traffic spike from data-driven audience segment in the 30 days following each independent review publication. Measure referral traffic from review sites and forums
metrics.target_threshold: >3x traffic spike from review referral sources in 30 days post-review; >4% conversion rate from review traffic
metrics.failure_threshold: <2x traffic spike — review did not generate significant interest; review may have been negative or RS1 data performance was underwhelming
metrics.data_source: GA4 referral traffic analysis, Shopify conversion by source, review publication tracking

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Let confidence-mechanism framework emerge organically from testimonials

metrics.metric_name: Organic Confidence Mention Rate in Testimonials
metrics.measurement_method: Track the percentage of customer testimonials, reviews, and survey responses that organically use 'confidence' or confidence-adjacent language (certain, trust, calm, assured) when describing their RS1 experience — without being prompted with confidence framing
metrics.target_threshold: >60% of testimonials organically mention confidence by Month 6 — validates that confidence IS the experienced outcome
metrics.failure_threshold: <40% — confidence is not the dominant experienced outcome; the mechanism-to-confidence chain may not be as strong as research suggested
metrics.data_source: Review content analysis, post-purchase survey open-ended responses, customer interview transcripts

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Monitor Stage 5 identity/tribal dynamics for future layering

metrics.measurement_method: Track emergence of RS1-specific community behaviors: dedicated forum threads, user-created content, repeat purchase indicators, 'RS1 family' type language in reviews, GAMERS declarations ('switched and never going back'). This is a monitoring metric, not an actionable KPI at launch
metrics.target_threshold: >5 organic community indicators by Month 6 (forum threads, user videos, social groups)
metrics.failure_threshold: <2 — no tribal identity emerging; Stage 5 positioning is premature; continue monitoring only
metrics.data_source: Social listening, forum monitoring, review sentiment analysis, customer community platform analytics

opportunity_metrics.opportunity_summary: Cross-cutting Product Performance Metrics (applies to all opportunities)

metrics.measurement_method: Track pre-launch waitlist signups, email opt-ins from RS1 teaser content, 'notify me' clicks, and social ad engagement rates during the 2-week pre-launch period. These leading signals predict launch-week revenue performance
metrics.target_threshold: >2,000 waitlist signups by launch day; >5% email CTR on pre-launch teaser sequence
metrics.failure_threshold: <1,000 waitlist signups — pre-launch demand signals are weak; consider delaying launch or increasing pre-launch content investment
metrics.data_source: Klaviyo waitlist flow, landing page opt-in tracking, Meta Ads pre-launch campaign metrics

metrics.measurement_method: Track total RS1 revenue (heel-shaft + center-shaft) against pre-launch forecast. Break down by SKU, channel (DTC web, email, paid), and customer segment (warm list vs cold traffic)
metrics.target_threshold: >90% of revenue forecast achieved by Month 3
metrics.failure_threshold: <70% of forecast — fundamental positioning or product-market-fit issue; trigger strategic review
metrics.data_source: Shopify/ecommerce revenue dashboard, financial reporting

metrics.measurement_method: Track return rate and exchange rate (heel-shaft to center-shaft or vice versa) as percentage of units sold. Monitor return reasons via customer service categorization
metrics.target_threshold: <8% return rate (premium golf equipment average is 10-12%)
metrics.failure_threshold: >12% — product or messaging expectation mismatch; investigate top return reasons immediately
metrics.data_source: Shopify returns data, customer service ticket categorization, RMA tracking system

metrics.measurement_method: Deploy NPS survey at 30 days and 90 days post-purchase. Track NPS score, promoter/detractor ratio, and verbatim comments
metrics.target_threshold: >60 NPS at 30 days; >65 NPS at 90 days (improving over time confirms the putter is not a honeymoon product)
metrics.warning_threshold: 40-60 NPS at 30 days; 40-65 NPS at 90 days
metrics.failure_threshold: <40 NPS at 30 days OR NPS declines by >10 points between 30-day and 90-day surveys — product is experiencing honeymoon effect; messaging is overpromising
metrics.data_source: NPS survey tool (Delighted, AskNicely, or Klaviyo survey flow), customer feedback analysis

metrics.measurement_method: Track blended and channel-specific CPA: Meta Ads, Google Ads, YouTube Ads, email (cost = list maintenance + content production), organic (cost = content production). Compare to target CPA based on LTV assumptions
metrics.target_threshold: <$80 blended CPA (assuming 20% contribution margin at $399)
metrics.failure_threshold: >$120 blended CPA — unit economics are unsustainable; optimize creative, funnel, or targeting before scaling spend
metrics.data_source: Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, attribution platform (Triple Whale, Northbeam, or equivalent)

metrics.metric_name: SKU Mix: Heel-Shaft vs Center-Shaft Ratio
metrics.measurement_method: Track the ratio of heel-shaft ($399) to center-shaft ($429) sales. The heel-shaft option is RS1's key differentiator vs LAB (who only offers center-shaft). Monitor shifts in ratio over time
metrics.target_threshold: >55% heel-shaft sales (confirms differentiation from LAB is working)
metrics.failure_threshold: <40% heel-shaft — buyers are choosing the center-shaft variant that is more LAB-comparable; heel-shaft differentiation messaging needs strengthening
metrics.data_source: Shopify SKU-level sales data, product analytics

metrics.measurement_method: Track self-reported or Arccos-verified average putts per round improvement at 30, 60, and 90 days post-purchase. Use post-purchase survey and follow-up emails
metrics.target_threshold: >2.5 putts per round average improvement at 90 days
metrics.failure_threshold: <1 putt per round improvement — product performance is not meeting marketing claims; reevaluate mechanism effectiveness claims
metrics.data_source: Post-purchase survey, Arccos integration data, beta tester tracking

metrics.measurement_method: Track overall landing page conversion rate (visit to purchase) for the RS1 product page, broken down by traffic source
metrics.target_threshold: >3.5% overall conversion rate; >5% for warm email traffic; >1.5% for cold paid traffic
metrics.failure_threshold: <2% overall — fundamental funnel issue; investigate page flow, proof placement, and offer structure
metrics.data_source: GA4, Shopify analytics, landing page platform analytics

metrics.metric_name: Email List RS1 Launch Sequence Performance
metrics.measurement_method: Track the entire RS1 launch email sequence: open rates, click rates, conversion rates per email. Identify the highest- and lowest-performing emails to optimize sequence structure
metrics.target_threshold: >30% average open rate across sequence; >2.5% average CTR; >0.5% per-email conversion rate
metrics.failure_threshold: <20% open — list fatigue or subject line failure; <1.5% CTR — email content not compelling; <0.3% conversion — offer or proof gap in email copy
metrics.data_source: Klaviyo email analytics dashboard

metrics.measurement_method: Track number of YouTube creator seedings shipped, reviews published, aggregate view count, and sentiment of reviews. Monitor for 'Let It Fall' moment inclusion in creator content
metrics.target_threshold: >10 creator reviews published by Month 2; >500K aggregate views; >80% positive sentiment
metrics.failure_threshold: <5 reviews published OR <60% positive sentiment — creator pipeline is stalled or product is not impressing reviewers; investigate and course-correct seeding strategy
metrics.data_source: YouTube search monitoring, creator relationship tracking spreadsheet, social listening

metrics.metric_name: Paid Media ROAS by Creative Concept
metrics.measurement_method: Track return on ad spend broken down by creative concept: McGinley origin, Let It Fall demo, self-blame liberation, honeymoon explanation, evolutionary narrative, LAB comparison, price wedge. Identify which opportunity messaging drives the most efficient acquisition
metrics.target_threshold: >3.0x blended ROAS; identify at least 2 creative concepts at >4.0x ROAS
metrics.failure_threshold: <2.0x blended ROAS — paid media is not profitable; pause scaling, optimize creative, or shift budget to higher-ROAS channels
metrics.data_source: Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, attribution platform

decision_triggers.condition: Return rate exceeds 12% in any 2-week period AND NPS at 30 days is below 40
decision_triggers.action: HALT paid media scaling. Conduct emergency product-messaging alignment review. Survey all returners for specific complaint patterns. If returns are mechanism-related (not sizing/preference), pause all face-stability claims pending investigation

decision_triggers.condition: McGinley-lead cold traffic ads underperform brand-lead ads by >20% on CPA after 2 weeks of testing
decision_triggers.action: Abandon McGinley-lead cold traffic strategy. Test mechanism-lead creative (Let It Fall demo as hero) for cold traffic instead. Reserve McGinley content for mid-funnel credibility building

decision_triggers.condition: CPA exceeds $120 on any paid channel for 7+ consecutive days with spend >$500/day
decision_triggers.action: Pause scaling on that channel. Test 3 new creative variants before resuming spend. If CPA remains >$120 after creative refresh, reallocate budget to higher-performing channels

decision_triggers.condition: Competitor (TaylorMade, Odyssey, Ping, or Callaway) announces a forward-CG or forward-weighted putter
decision_triggers.action: Activate competitive response protocol: (1) Accelerate 'first mover' content creation. (2) Deploy the 'Let It Fall' challenge against competitor product within 72 hours of availability. (3) Emphasize McGinley's 25-year pedigree vs competitor's rushed entry. (4) Shift messaging from 'introducing' to 'pioneering' forward weighting

decision_triggers.condition: LAB Golf launches a heel-shaft variant OR drops prices below $399
decision_triggers.action: Escalate OPP-002 competitive response: (1) If heel-shaft: shift messaging to RS1's steel face feel and zero adaptation period as remaining differentiators. (2) If price drop: shift price-wedge messaging to feature-set superiority and total value rather than price advantage

decision_triggers.condition: Face drift term is used by a competitor in their marketing materials
decision_triggers.action: Accelerate trademark filing if not already submitted. Intensify face drift vocabulary deployment across all channels. Create 'We coined it' origin content establishing RS1 as the source. Monitor whether competitor is using the term in RS1's frame or redefining it

decision_triggers.condition: NPS declines by >10 points between 30-day and 90-day surveys (indicating honeymoon fade)
decision_triggers.action: Emergency investigation: (1) Deploy detailed 90-day survey probing putting performance consistency. (2) Analyze Arccos/ShotScope data from beta testers at 90 days for actual performance degradation. (3) If confirmed, pause all 'carousel-breaker' and 'permanent physics' messaging until root cause identified. (4) If NOT confirmed (NPS decline due to other factors), investigate customer experience issues beyond product performance

decision_triggers.condition: Beta tester 10-round data shows <1.0 average putts-per-round improvement across cohort
decision_triggers.action: Do NOT publish weak beta data. Extend beta period to 20 rounds. If still <1.0 improvement at 20 rounds, pivot all messaging from specific performance claims to 'consistency and feel' framing. Halt any planned quantified improvement claims in advertising

decision_triggers.condition: Self-blame reframe ads generate >5% negative comment rate (comments expressing offense, anger, or dismissal)
decision_triggers.action: Pause self-blame reframe creative on that platform. Test softened versions: 'What if it wasn't your stroke?' instead of 'Your stroke was never the problem.' If softened versions also generate >5% negative comments, deprioritize the reframe and lead with mechanism demonstration instead

decision_triggers.condition: Heel-shaft SKU represents <30% of total sales after 30 days
decision_triggers.action: Investigate why RS1's key differentiator (heel-shaft option) is underperforming. Options: (1) Heel-shaft education content is insufficient — deploy dedicated heel-shaft vs center-shaft comparison content. (2) Audience is more LAB-familiar than expected and defaulting to center-shaft comfort. (3) Consider A/B landing page test leading with heel-shaft as default

decision_triggers.condition: YouTube creator reviews are >40% negative or neutral sentiment (out of first 10 reviews)
decision_triggers.action: Pause creator seeding program. Analyze negative review themes: (1) Product performance — investigate specific complaints, share with McGinley. (2) Aesthetics/feel — this is a perception issue, not mechanism issue. (3) Comparison to current gamer — may indicate product-market fit issue. Course-correct product or messaging before resuming seeding

decision_triggers.condition: MyGolfSpy independent testing shows RS1 face-angle stability is not in the top 3 of tested putters
decision_triggers.action: Do NOT contest the data publicly. Shift messaging: (1) Deprioritize quantified face-angle claims. (2) Emphasize 'designed-in natural pendulum' and 'zero adaptation period' differentiators that testing may not capture. (3) Accelerate customer testimonial collection to provide experiential proof to supplement unfavorable data. (4) Commission alternative testing methodology that better captures RS1's advantages

decision_triggers.condition: Email warm list launch sequence achieves <0.3% per-email conversion rate across first 5 emails
decision_triggers.action: Warm list is not converting at expected rates. Diagnose: (1) Check if PG's existing customer base overlaps with RS1 target WEB (5-15 handicap frustrated ball-strikers). (2) Test segment-specific versions of the launch sequence for instruction buyers vs equipment buyers. (3) If PG list overlap is poor, accelerate cold traffic scaling and reduce reliance on warm list revenue projections

decision_triggers.condition: Blended ROAS drops below 2.0x for 14+ consecutive days with spend >$2,000/day
decision_triggers.action: Paid media is not profitable at scale. Execute: (1) Cut spend to breakeven level. (2) Test creative refresh with 5 new concepts in 7 days. (3) Evaluate whether the issue is creative fatigue, audience saturation, or fundamental product-market-fit. (4) If ROAS does not recover above 2.5x within 21 days of creative refresh, reallocate budget to retention/referral programs

decision_triggers.condition: Face drift organic mentions exceed 50 by Month 3 AND search volume exceeds 200/month
decision_triggers.action: Vocabulary ownership is succeeding — ACCELERATE. Execute: (1) Create 'Face Drift Guide' cornerstone content for SEO ownership. (2) Deploy face drift education ad campaign to capture growing search intent. (3) Explore trademark registration for 'Face Drift' as a branded term. (4) Increase face drift vocabulary deployment in all new content

decision_triggers.condition: 90-day NPS is >65 AND 90-day retention is >85% AND organic confidence mentions are >60%
decision_triggers.action: All product-performance indicators are strong — ESCALATE investment. Execute: (1) Increase paid media budget by 50%. (2) Launch referral program leveraging high NPS. (3) Begin development of RS1 v2 or product line extension. (4) Initiate Stage 5 identity messaging ('For golfers who trust physics')

decision_triggers.condition: Zero organic face drift mentions AND <50 branded searches/week at Week 4
decision_triggers.action: Category messaging is failing to penetrate. Execute: (1) Test alternative root cause terminology ('face twist,' 'putter rotation,' 'face wander') in A/B ad creative. (2) Increase demonstration video distribution spend by 100%. (3) Deploy YouTube creator challenge: 'Do the Let It Fall test with your current putter.' (4) If no terminology gains traction within 60 days, abandon vocabulary ownership strategy and lead with mechanism demonstration only

decision_triggers.condition: RS1 content uses competitor vocabulary ('zero torque,' 'lie angle balance,' 'face balanced') in any published material
decision_triggers.action: Vocabulary discipline violation. Execute: (1) Remove competitor vocabulary from published content within 24 hours. (2) Retrain all content creators using the copy review checklist. (3) Audit all existing content for additional violations. (4) This is CCR-004 mitigation — if the market groups RS1 with ZT putters, positioning fails
